turns.content: What brings you in today?

I've been feeling really dizzy when I stand up, and yesterday I almost fell. My wife also noticed that my hand shakes sometimes, and that's worrying me. So I thought I should come and check what's going on.
When did this start?
The dizziness started maybe about three weeks ago. The shaking in my hand, my wife pointed that out just last week, but I think it might have been there a little longer without me really noticing.
Can you describe the pain or discomfort? Where exactly do you feel it?

Well, the dizziness isn't really a pain. It's more like the room spins a little when I get up from a chair or bed. Sometimes I also get a dull headache around my forehead. And my legs feel a bit numb and tired a lot.
Have you had any fever or chills?
No, no fever or chills at all. Just the dizziness, the headache, and feeling tired. Nothing like being sick with a cold or the flu.
What medications are you currently taking?
I take metoprolol every day for my blood pressure and heart, and atorvastatin for my cholesterol. I've been on these for a few years now, and I never miss a dose.
Do you have any chronic medical conditions?
Yes, I have coronary artery disease and high blood pressure. My doctor told me those are under control with my medicines, but I still worry about them, especially now with these new problems.
Are you allergic to any medications?
No, I'm not allergic to any medicine that I know of. I've taken a few different ones over the years, and never had a bad reaction.
Have you tried anything to relieve the symptoms?
I've been trying to sit down slowly and waiting a bit before standing up, but it doesn't always help. Sometimes I drink more water or eat a little salty snack, but the dizziness still comes back.
Does anything make it better or worse?

It gets worse when I stand up quickly, especially in the morning or after sitting for a long time. Lying down or sitting still makes it feel a bit better, but the dizziness goes away after a few minutes. The headache never really stops, though.
How has this been affecting your daily life?

It's made me slow down a lot. I used to walk in the garden every morning, but now I'm worried about falling. My wife keeps an eye on me all the time, and I don't like that feeling. I also feel tired most afternoons, so I end up resting more than I'd like.
Do you smoke or drink alcohol?
I quit smoking about ten years ago, and I only have a glass of wine maybe once a month, if that. My wife says it's better for my heart to avoid it, so I really don't drink much.
Has anyone in your family had similar problems?

My father had heart problems too, but I'm not sure if he ever felt dizzy like this. He had a stroke later in life. Nobody else in my family had shaking hands or numbness like I have now, as far as I know.
Have you experienced anything like this before?
No, nothing like this before. I've had occasional headaches, but never this dizziness or the shaking in my hand. This whole thing feels new and honestly a bit scary for me.
I've explained quite a bit — does that make sense so far?
Yes, I think I understand most of it. But could you explain again what might be causing the dizziness and the shaking together? I get confused when you use big words, sorry.
On a scale of 1 to 10, how would you rate what you're going through right now — physically, and also emotionally?

Physically, it's maybe a 3 out of 10. Not terrible pain, but the dizziness really bothers me. Emotionally, I'd say it's more like a 7 out of 10. I'm worried all the time because I don't know what's causing this, and I don't want to fall or have something bad happen to my heart.
Do you have any questions for me?

Yes, I do have a couple of questions. First, do you think this dizziness could be from my heart condition or the medicines I take? And second, should I be worried about having a stroke like my father did? I just want to know if I'm okay.
What do you think might be going on?
Honestly, I'm not sure. I hope it's not something serious like a stroke or a heart problem. Maybe it's just old age or the medicines causing this? But I really don't know. That's why I came to you.
Is there anything else you think I should know?

Now that you ask, my wife also mentioned that sometimes my hand feels a bit numb or tingling, not just shaking. That happens maybe once or twice a day, but it goes away quickly. I almost forgot to say that.
